brief_summary: Two-column ledger. Left (bronze solid, 'earns its keep'): lightweight metal cases that ring, aggressive typists who shift a light board, a finishing move on an already-tuned board. Right (bordeaux dashed, 'does not help'): hollow resonance from an empty cavity, scratchy switches or untuned stabilizers, flex complaints on a rigid mount. Each card carries the reason for its verdict.
data_sources: brass-weight-mod.mdx § 'Where it earns its keep' — 'The mod makes the most sense on lightweight, thin-walled metal cases ... It also makes sense for typists who bottom out hard and consistently shift a light board across the desk'

brass-weight-mod.mdx § 'What it does not fix' — 'A brass weight will not fix a scratchy switch, will not quiet down untuned stabilizers ... will not turn a hollow-sounding budget board into a dense-sounding one ... it also will not compensate for a genuinely bad mounting design'
brass-weight-mod.mdx § 'Where it earns its keep' — 'It is a finishing move for builders who have already done the cheap, high-leverage mods'
